Record-002: Source is from LittlewoodRichardson.tex, lemma lem.sf.alternant-0. It has two parts: (a) if α has two equal entries, then a_α = 0; (b) swapping two entries gives a_β = -a_α. The source_progress_context shows a prior same-label declaration: alternant_zero_of_eq which formalizes part (a). So we should formalize part (b) only, as per instruction: "If an earlier declaration already formalizes one part, prefer the remaining or next source part instead of bundling all parts." So we need to formalize part (b): Let β be obtained from α by swapping two entries. Then a_β = -a_α.

We have local context: variable N : ℕ [NeZero N], variable R [CommRing R], alternant definition (sum over permutations). There is also alternant_zero_of_eq already defined. So we need to state a theorem about swapping two entries. The source says "Let β∈ℕ^N be an N-tuple obtained from α by swapping two entries." So we need to define β as α composed with a transposition. We can use Equiv.swap. So β = α ∘ (swap i j) for some i≠j. Then we need to show alternant (R:=R) N (α ∘ (swap i j)) = - alternant (R:=R) N α.

Proof sketch: Use the definition of alternant as sum over σ: sign(σ) xPow(α∘σ). Then swapping two entries in α corresponds to composing with a transposition. We can use that the sum over σ of sign(σ) xPow(α∘σ) is antisymmetric under swapping entries. More concretely, we can use that the alternant is defined as ∑_σ sign(σ) xPow(α∘σ). Then alternant (α∘τ) = ∑_σ sign(σ) xPow(α∘τ∘σ) = ∑_σ sign(σ) xPow(α∘(τ∘σ)). By change of variable σ' = τ∘σ, we get ∑_σ' sign(τ⁻¹∘σ') xPow(α∘σ') = sign(τ) ∑_σ' sign(σ') xPow(α∘σ') = sign(τ) alternant α. Since sign(swap) = -1, we get -alternant α. So we need to use the property that sign of a transposition is -1. There is lemma Equiv.Perm.sign_swap. Also we need to use that the sum over σ is invariant under reindexing by a fixed permutation. That is, ∑_σ f(τ∘σ) = ∑_σ f(σ) because τ is a bijection. So we can use Finset.sum_equiv or Finset.sum_bij.

We need to use the local definition of alternant: noncomputable def alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R := ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin N), Equiv.Perm.sign σ • xPow (α ∘ σ). So we can work with that.

We also need to use xPow definition: noncomputable abbrev xPow (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R := AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.monomialExp α. But we may not need its properties, just that it is a function of α.

source_focus_summary: "Lemma lem.det.minors-diag: determinant of minors of a diagonal matrix. Part (a): det(sub_P^P D) = ∏_{i∈P} d_i. Part (b): for distinct P,Q with |P|=|Q|, det(sub_P^Q D)=0."

selected_source_part: "Whole lemma lem.det.minors-diag (both parts (a) and (b))"

source_part_rationale: "No prior same-label declaration exists; the source span label covers the entire lemma."

formalization_sketch: list of steps.

We'll write:

[
  "Define D := Matrix.diagonal d where d : Fin n → R.",
  "Part (a): For any P : Finset (Fin n), det (sub[P,P] D) = ∏ i in P, d i.",
  "  Proof: sub[P,P] D is diagonal with entries d (P.orderEmbOfFin rfl i) for i:Fin P.card. Its determinant is product of these entries, which equals ∏ i∈P d i because orderEmbOfFin is a bijection onto P.",
  "  Use lemma Matrix.det_diagonal and Finset.prod_image or Finset.prod_bij.",
  "Part (b): For P Q : Finset (Fin n), P ≠ Q, hcard : P.card = Q.card, then det (sub[P,Q] D) = 0.",
  "  Proof: Since P ≠ Q and |P|=|Q|, there exists i ∈ P \\ Q (or i ∈ Q \\ P). Choose such i. Then for all j, sub[P,Q] D i j = D (P.orderEmbOfFin rfl i) (Q.orderEmbOfFin rfl j) = 0 because D is diagonal and the row index is in P\\Q while column indices are in Q, so they are never equal. Hence the i-th row is zero, so determinant zero by Matrix.det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero.",
  "  Use Finset.exists_of_card_eq_and_ne to get the existence of i in P\\Q."
]

Simpler: by_contra h; apply hne; apply Finset.Subset.antisymm? We can use: have hsub : P ⊆ Q := Finset.subset_of_forall? Not exactly. We can use: if P \\ Q = ∅ then P ⊆ Q, and since |P|=|Q|, then P=Q. So we can use Finset.card_eq_card_of_subset? There is lemma Finset.card_le_card_of_subset. So we can write:

have hsub : P ⊆ Q := by
  intro x hx; by_contra hx'; apply Finset.not_mem_sdiff_of_mem_right hx' hx; exact Finset.sdiff_eq_empty_iff_subset.mp ?_ 
But we can use Finset.sdiff_eq_empty_iff_subset. So we can get that if P \\ Q = ∅ then P ⊆ Q. Then using hcard, we get P = Q, contradiction. So P \\ Q ≠ ∅. Then we can pick i ∈ P \\ Q.

We need to use Finset.sdiff_eq_empty_iff_subset. That is a lemma.

Also we need to use Matrix.det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero. That lemma expects a row index i such that for all j, M i j = 0. So we need to provide that.
